evals.name: latin-extension-french
evals.prompt: We've fine-tuned Llama-3 on French. Fertility is 1.7× English baseline. Should we extend the vocab?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) note ratio is in 1.5-2.0 'mild' range; (2) recommend OPTIONAL extension — depends on cost sensitivity (latency-sensitive yes; budget-tight maybe not); (3) for French specifically, FOCUS is the right method (same script, high overlap with English vocab); (4) NOT recommend mandatory extension for this ratio.

evals.name: agglutinative-turkish-mandatory
evals.prompt: I'm seeing Llama-3 produce 2.4× the tokens for Turkish vs English. Is this a problem?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) confirm 2.4× is HIGH (in 2.0-3.0 range); (2) flag Turkish as agglutinative (morphology-driven fertility); (3) recommend vocab extension; (4) suggest OFA (Turkish has parallel data) or FOCUS for shared Latin script; (5) note byte-fallback should already be on; (6) explain cost — 2.4× tokens means 2.4× inference cost, halved effective context window.

evals.name: abugida-khmer-tokenizer-config
evals.prompt: We're training a SentencePiece tokenizer for Khmer from scratch. What hyperparameters?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) set character_coverage=0.99999 (NOT 0.9995); (2) byte_fallback=true MANDATORY; (3) Unigram LM > BPE for low-resource; (4) add_dummy_prefix=false (Khmer is space-less); (5) note word segmentation needs to be done upstream (Khmer has no spaces between words); (6) vocab_size around 30K-50K depending on data.

evals.name: ideographic-mandarin-coverage
evals.prompt: For a Mandarin LLM, my SentencePiece config has character_coverage=0.9995. The model is bad at proper names. Why?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) diagnose: 0.9995 drops 0.05% of characters which includes thousands of rare names; (2) recommend 0.99999 for Han script; (3) recommend byte_fallback=true; (4) note vocab_size should be 100K+ for Han; (5) add_dummy_prefix=false (Han is space-less); (6) explain that name-handling is the canonical symptom of low coverage on ideographic scripts.

evals.name: mixed-script-hinglish-strategy
evals.prompt: Our chatbot needs to handle Hinglish (Hindi-English code-switching) — text sometimes in Devanagari, sometimes in Latin transliteration. How should we structure the tokenizer?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recognize this as a mixed-script tokenization problem; (2) train a single tokenizer covering BOTH scripts (don't use separate tokenizers); (3) byte_fallback=true MANDATORY (mixed scripts → unpredictable input); (4) higher vocab_size (~80K) to cover both Devanagari and Latin Hindi adequately; (5) consider language tags `<script_deva>` `<script_latn>` as user-defined symbols; (6) audit fertility per script after training, not just aggregate.
